att: Hector Gonzalez, Pascal Walgien, Elva, Jazin, Tanika, Sameer (partial attendee list from updates – likely more)
dis: Company and market update; review of volume and financials; discussed digitization of reporting, escalation processes, quality of invoicing, and SAP BNL integration; sustainability and EcoVadis silver medal; regional improvement plans for EMEIA, NAM, APAC, ME; main themes: documentation accuracy, lead time adherence, continuous improvement.
act:
Clean up old invoices (bi-weekly call for Italy to address outstanding invoice issues).
Align and digitize regional reports, review dashboard requirements.
Improve timely sharing of quarterly rate updates in Europe.
Roll out SAP TMS system implementation for KSA/ME region.
Review and align on booking milestone handling with NAM team.
Share breakdown of MISC charges separately.
Escalation tree to be followed within PSA BDP for issue handling.
Quarterly rate updates to be shared on time, with next planned update on 2025-11-18.
Italy bi-weekly call set up to clean up old invoices.
Monthly consolidated billing and improved invoice accuracy for Middle East operations.
SAP TM update to reflect correct vessel details in NAM region.
Continuous Improvement Project for KPIs; tracking bookings and documentation accuracy.
Auto-send documents to PSA BDP via SAP; project with SAP architect.
Carbon Dashboard Phase 2 planning for launch in mid 2025.

att: Todd Bratten, Landon McNaspy, Matt Coker, Derek Allen, Tim Mayo
dis: Discussion on sales trends, volumes and activity for trucking services. Review of KPIs related to driver compliance, load acceptance, safety, maintenance, and delivery performance. Action items on TMS compliance, maintenance fees, and driver count adjustment.
act:
Weekly PM Report to include Nouryon Trailer numbers and status.
Ruan to start adding tank wash fees to freight orders after Gilberto's approval; not to be added to Maintenance POs. Effective June 15th, 2025.
Columbus maintenance fees charged against Columbus PO effective immediately for assets not related to HP trailers.
Delay close order status to 23:59 of same day; effective immediately.
Nouryon to decrease web settle days to 5 days to help with freight cost accrual issue; Landon to follow up.
Ruan to decrease the driver count to 11 full time drivers week of June 23rd for 2-3 weeks; Nouryon to decide whether to bring driver count back to 12 or keep at 11.
Weekly PM Report to include Nouryon Trailer #’s and status of trailers out in the body of the email. Todd/Mark to advise on effective date.
Ruan to start adding tank wash fees to freight orders after approved by Gilberto via email. No tank wash fees on Maintenance PO’s Effective June 15th 2025.
Columbus Maintenance fees will be charged against the Columbus PO effective immediately (includes any Columbus site assets not related to HP trailers). Chris Crawford and Michael Bailey are your point of contacts.
Ruan to delay the close status to 23:59 of the same day. Please make effective immediately.
Nouryon to decrease the web settle days to 5 days to help with freight cost accrual issue. Landon following up.
Driver count-Ruan to decrease the driver count to 11 full time drivers the week of June 23rd for 2-3 weeks. During this period Nouryon will decide whether to bring the driver back to 12 or reduce to 11 drivers.
Set up Ruan/Nouryon weekly call starting 06/16/2026.
Ruan/Nouryon Weekly call-Landon to set up the weekly meeting starting 06/16.

att: Pascal Walgien, Nouryon team, PSA BDP team
dis: Business and project updates, volume overview, global market update, financial overdue discussion, discussion of volume and KPI trends, process improvement opportunities highlighted for invoicing, routing compliance, and process automation.
act:
Timely invoicing KSA; invoicing desk created and closer monitored. KSA warehousing invoicing process re-defined and agreed with Nouryon.
ISF sending – new reports in place to cover any missed; NL Process gap analysis to be discussed with Nouryon.
Root cause analysis training held for PSA BDP Operations team to assist in assignment of non-conformances.
Carrier escalation contacts received from Nouryon to facilitate better response times.
Haz approval process timeliness improvement: add approval timeline to radar for visibility.
Root cause analysis training held for PSA BDP Operations team to assist in assignment of non-conformances

New tender in place - could affect BKC KPI for Q2
KSA warehousing invoicing process re-defined and agreed with Nouryon
ISF sending – new reports are in place to cover any missed
NL Process gap analysis to be discussed with Nouryon
